items.role: Always-on 3.3 V linear regulator — sources the LPC_VCC rail that powers the System Controller before any other rail is enabled; loss of this output leaves the entire board dead with no UART response.
typical_failure_modes: output shorted to GND (leaky die)
open output — LPC_VCC absent, D11 never illuminates
input–output short under overvoltage event
cold joint causing intermittent LPC_VCC dropout
items.notes: Probe output in diode-mode for short before replacing; if output reads low-ohms to GND, isolate downstream loads (U18 and others on LPC_VCC) by lifting the output side to determine whether short is in U14 itself or a downstream consumer. Sources: https://mntre.com/documentation/reform-handbook/advanced.html

items.role: NXP LPC11U24 ARM Cortex-M0 System Controller MCU — sequences all power rails via GPIO, monitors battery cells over SPI, and exposes a UART command interface; GPIO damage or loss of LPC_VCC supply prevents any rail from turning on.
typical_failure_modes: GPIO output damaged by reversed-battery overvoltage — rails fail to switch on
SPI bus corruption causing incorrect cell-voltage readings
cold joint on supply pins causing intermittent boot failure
latch-up on I/O pins from ESD during rework
items.notes: Confirm LPC_VCC = 3.3 V at J22 pin 15 before suspecting U18 itself. UART responsiveness and D11 LED state are the primary in-circuit health checks. Sources: https://mntre.com/documentation/reform-handbook/advanced.html, https://community.mnt.re/t/debugging-dead-motherboard/970

items.role: Texas Instruments TUSB8041 4-port USB 3.0 hub IC — expands CPU-module USB controllers to five external ports; requires 1.2 V supply to enumerate; loss of VCC_1V2 kills all USB ports.
typical_failure_modes: non-functional when VCC_1V2 rail is absent or low
BGA/QFN cold joint causing intermittent USB enumeration failures
ESD damage on USB data lines — one or more ports dead
items.notes: Probe C37 for 1.2 V as a first step before suspecting U9 die failure. If 1.2 V is present and USB is still dead, inspect solder joints under microscope. Sources: https://mntre.com/reform2/handbook/advanced.html

items.role: Texas Instruments SN65DSI86 MIPI-DSI to eDP bridge IC — converts DSI signals from the CPU SOM to eDP for the internal display panel; requires 1.2 V supply and a seated J24 flex; failure causes dark or flickering internal display while HDMI remains unaffected.
typical_failure_modes: cold joint on supply or signal pins — intermittent or dead display
non-functional when VCC_1V2 rail is absent
BGA/QFN ball crack under thermal cycling causing permanent display loss
ESD damage on DSI lanes from hot-plugging flex cable
items.notes: Reflow U10 with flux if cold joint is suspected. Verify 1.2 V at C37 and 3V3_PWR_AUX before reflowing. Also inspect J24 ZIF pads under microscope for lifted contacts — a mis-seated flex is more common than a failed IC. Sources: https://mntre.com/reform2/handbook/advanced.html, https://community.mnt.re/t/edp-cable-or-board-issue/3898

items.role: Mini-PCIe slot connector — carries the primary PCIe interface from the i.MX8M SOM to a Wi-Fi or other PCIe card; PCIE1_PWR_EN GPIO from U18 must be asserted to power the slot.
typical_failure_modes: mechanical wear on connector pins causing intermittent PCIe card detection
cold joint on connector through-hole or SMD feet after board flex
PCIE1_PWR absent if U18 GPIO damaged — card never powers up
items.notes: PCIE1_PWR is controlled by U18; confirm the System Controller is functional before blaming U11 connector hardware. Sources: https://mntre.com/documentation/reform-handbook/hardware.html

items.role: Voltage level translator for the SD card interface — bridges 3.3 V and 1.8 V logic domains; completely non-functional if VCC_1V8 is absent, blocking SD boot.
typical_failure_modes: non-functional when VCC_1V8 rail is absent — SD interface blocked
ESD damage on data lines — SD reads fail with rails present
cold joint on supply pins causing intermittent SD errors
items.notes: Probe C130 for 1.8 V before suspecting U28 die failure. If 1.8 V is absent, trace back to the 1.8 V buck regulator output and check in diode-mode for a short on the output rail. Sources: https://mntre.com/reform2/handbook/advanced.html

items.role: Voltage level translator for the SD card interface — bridges 3.3 V and 1.8 V logic domains alongside U28; completely non-functional if VCC_1V8 is absent, blocking SD boot.
typical_failure_modes: non-functional when VCC_1V8 rail is absent — SD interface blocked
ESD damage on data lines — SD reads fail with rails present
cold joint on supply pins causing intermittent SD errors
items.notes: Probe C130 for 1.8 V before suspecting U8 die failure. If 1.8 V is absent, diode-mode probe nearby decoupling caps to detect short on the rail before reflowing or replacing the 1.8 V regulator. Sources: https://mntre.com/reform2/handbook/advanced.html

items.role: Analog Devices LTC4020 buck/boost regulator and LiFePO4 charger — produces the ~29 V main system bus from a 9–32 V barrel-jack input and manages charging/balancing of eight LiFePO4 cells; failure or misconfiguration kills the entire system bus.
typical_failure_modes: hardware-level balancing path defect on R-2 boards — cells charge unevenly (requires component-level rework modification)
output short to GND — main 29 V bus collapses
cold joint on high-current switching pins under thermal cycling
open bootstrap or feedback resistor causing regulator drop-out
items.notes: R-2 production boards had a known hardware balancing issue; consult MNT R-2 motherboard update from April 2021 (https://community.mnt.re/t/mnt-reform-r-2-motherboard-update-2021-04-19/177) for the specific component modification. LTC6803 SPI data should be verified if balancing readings appear incorrect.

items.role: Linear Technology LTC6803 multicell battery stack monitor — measures individual LiFePO4 cell voltages over SPI and reports to U18 (System Controller); SPI signal integrity issues can corrupt cell-voltage readings.
typical_failure_modes: SPI bus fault causing corrupted or stuck cell-voltage readings
cold joint on SPI or cell-sense pins after board flex
ESD damage on sense inputs from improper battery connection
items.notes: Confirm SPI signal integrity between LTC6803IG-4#PBF and U18 if balancing data appears wrong. No widespread die-level failures reported in community sources. Sources: https://community.mnt.re/t/mnt-reform-r-2-motherboard-update-2021-04-19/177

items.role: Main downstream buck regulators on the v3.0 motherboard — replace the older 5 V and 3.3 V converters to power the CPU SOM and system rails; instability on feedback pin or cold joints can cause rail hiccup after SOM swap.
typical_failure_modes: feedback pin instability causing oscillating or low output voltage
cold joint on inductor or output capacitor pads after rework
hiccup-mode triggering from excess capacitance on 5 V USB rail if output caps incorrectly specified
items.notes: Scope the feedback pin for oscillation if rail is low or unstable. The v3.0 board increased capacitance on 5 V USB rails — verify no cold joints on the new converters after SOM swap. Sources: https://mntre.com/documentation/reform-handbook/hardware.html

items.role: SMD input fuse — over-current protection in series on the barrel-jack input rail before R49; if blown, the board shows no rail activity whatsoever.
typical_failure_modes: open (blown) fuse from input over-current or reverse-polarity event — board completely dead
cracked fuse body from mechanical stress — intermittent open
cold joint on fuse pads causing resistive contact and voltage drop
items.notes: Check F1 in diode-mode or resistance mode; an open reading confirms blown fuse. If fuse is confirmed open, investigate root cause (short on input rail) before fitting replacement to avoid immediate re-blow. Sources: https://mntre.com/reform2/handbook/advanced.html

items.role: Sense resistor on the main power-input path — primary test point for measuring the incoming 9–32 V rail; probing across R49 with a DMM confirms whether input voltage has passed F1.
typical_failure_modes: open resistor from over-current event — input voltage absent downstream of F1
cold joint causing intermittent voltage reading at test point
pad lift after mechanical stress near barrel jack
items.notes: Probe R49 for input voltage (9–32 V nominal 24 V) with a DMM as the first step in a no-power diagnosis. If absent at R49, check F1 for continuity. Sources: https://mntre.com/reform2/handbook/advanced.html

items.role: Barrel-jack input connector — accepts 9–32 V (nominal 24 V) supply from the wall adapter; the entry point for all board power.
typical_failure_modes: cold joint on through-hole or SMD pins causing intermittent power loss
pad lift or cracked solder from mechanical stress of connector insertion
internal spring contact wear causing high-resistance connection
items.notes: If input voltage is absent at R49, visually inspect J1 solder joints and probe the connector pins directly before tracing further. Sources: https://mntre.com/reform2/handbook/advanced.html

items.role: Debug/UART connector — exposes LPC_VCC at pin 15 (key test point for 3.3 V always-on rail) and UART for System Controller command communication.
typical_failure_modes: cold joint on connector pins causing intermittent UART loss
damaged pin 15 contact giving false low LPC_VCC reading
connector body stress crack from repeated cable insertion
items.notes: Measure LPC_VCC at J22 pin 15 with a DMM (expected 3.3 V) as an early diagnostic step for System Controller issues. Sources: https://mntre.com/documentation/reform-handbook/advanced.html

items.role: 30-pin 0.5 mm pitch ZIF flex connector — carries MIPI-DSI signals from the CPU SOM to U10 (SN65DSI86 eDP bridge); pad lift or mis-seating causes intermittent or absent internal display.
typical_failure_modes: pad lift on ZIF pads after repeated cable reseating — intermittent display
torn or cracked flex contacts from improper cable removal
intermittent open after reassembly from mis-seated locking latch
ESD damage on DSI lanes from hot-plugging
items.notes: Inspect J24 ZIF pads under stereo microscope for lifted contacts after any reassembly. Re-seat or reflow as needed. Route Wi-Fi antenna cable away from DSI flex run to avoid interference. Sources: https://community.mnt.re/t/edp-cable-or-board-issue/3898, https://mntre.com/reform2/handbook/advanced.html

items.role: White LED status indicator — illuminates when LPC_VCC 3.3 V always-on rail is live; primary visual health indicator for the System Controller power domain.
typical_failure_modes: LED never lights — LPC_VCC absent (suspect U14 or short on rail)
LED extinguishes immediately after '1P' command — short on switched 3.3 V rail
open LED or cold joint giving false 'rail dead' impression
items.notes: D11 state is the primary first-pass indicator: lit = LPC_VCC present; extinguishes on '1P' = likely short on switched 3.3 V rail. Sources: https://mntre.com/documentation/reform-handbook/advanced.html, https://community.mnt.re/t/debugging-dead-motherboard/970

items.role: LED status indicator — illuminates when USB_PWR 5 V switched rail is active; key visual check for whether U18 has successfully enabled the 5 V path.
typical_failure_modes: LED never lights after '1P' command — 5 V switched rail absent (trace path from LTC4020 through switching FETs enabled by U18)
open LED or cold joint giving false 'rail dead' impression
items.notes: If D12 never illuminates after '1P' command, trace the 5 V path from LTC4020 output through the switching FETs controlled by U18. Sources: https://community.mnt.re/t/debugging-dead-motherboard/970

items.role: Decoupling capacitor on the 1.8 V rail — primary test point for measuring VCC_1V8; diode-mode probe on this cap detects shorts on the 1.8 V rail.
typical_failure_modes: shorted MLCC (X7R ceramic fracture) pulling VCC_1V8 to GND
cold joint on cap pads causing false low voltage reading at test point
items.notes: Probe C130 for 1.8 V as the first step in SD boot failure diagnosis. If rail is absent, diode-mode probe on C130 itself to check for a shorted cap. Sources: https://mntre.com/reform2/handbook/advanced.html

items.role: Decoupling capacitor on the 1.2 V rail — primary test point for measuring VCC_1V2; diode-mode probe on this cap detects shorts on the 1.2 V rail.
typical_failure_modes: shorted MLCC (ceramic fracture) pulling VCC_1V2 to GND — kills U9 and U10
cold joint on cap pads causing false low voltage reading at test point
items.notes: Probe C37 for 1.2 V as the first step when internal display is dark or USB ports are dead. If absent, diode-mode probe on nearby decoupling caps to locate the short before replacing the 1.2 V buck regulator. Sources: https://mntre.com/reform2/handbook/advanced.html

items.role: Micro-HDMI output connector on the Pocket Reform motherboard — provides external video output; susceptible to PCB pad lift under repeated mechanical stress from plug/unplug cycles.
typical_failure_modes: pad lift on solder joints from mechanical stress of repeated plug/unplug cycles
torn PCB pads leaving connector pins floating — no video output
cold joint under fine-pitch pins cracking after thermal cycling
items.notes: Under stereo microscope, clean lifted pads and reflow connector with flux and a fine-tip iron. If pads are torn, scrape clearance coat from exposed trace stubs and run 30 AWG jumpers from stubs to connector pins. Sources: https://community.mnt.re/t/tragedy-microhdmi-port-has-detached/4240
